title: [Bug]: View 'SCHEMATA' always shows default data instead of actual data.
poc:
SELECT
    ->   'def' AS CATALOG_NAME,
    ->   DATABASE_NAME AS SCHEMA_NAME,
    ->   'utf8mb4' AS DEFAULT_CHARACTER_SET_NAME,
    ->   b.COLLATION_NAME AS DEFAULT_COLLATION_NAME,
    ->   NULL AS SQL_PATH,
    ->   'NO' as DEFAULT_ENCRYPTION
    -> FROM
    ->   oceanbase.__all_database a
    ->   left join information_schema.COLLATIONS b ON a.collation_type = b.ID
    -> WHERE
    ->   a.tenant_id = 0 and in_recyclebin = 0 and database_name != '__recyclebin';
key_bug_features: View 'SCHEMATA' shows default data instead of actual data
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_schema (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_schema (id, name) VALUES (1, 'Test Schema 1');
INSERT INTO test_schema (id, name) VALUES (2, NULL);
SELECT * FROM test_schema WHERE name IS NULL;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_schema WHERE created_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '1 DAY';
